title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States to require that the Supreme Court of the United States be composed of nine justices.
plain_summary: This bill wants to change the Constitution to make sure the Supreme Court always has exactly nine judges. Right now, the Constitution doesn't say how many judges should be on the Supreme Court, so Congress could change that number if they wanted to. This new rule would lock in nine judges forever. To make this change, two-thirds of Congress and three-fourths of all the states would have to agree to it.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: This joint resolution proposes an amendment to the United States Constitution. The amendment would require the Supreme Court to always have exactly nine justices. For this amendment to become part of the Constitution, it must be approved by two-thirds of both the Senate and House of Representatives. Then it must be approved by the legislatures of three-fourths of all the states within seven years of being submitted to them for approval.
